# Basic MCP Weather Server Test
# Tests server startup, initialization, and basic tool discovery
# Note: These tests require OPENWEATHER_API_KEY to be set for full functionality

# Build the weather server binary
go build -o mcp-weather-server .

# Test without API key (should fail gracefully)
env OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=
exec mcp-weather-server 2>&1
stderr 'OPENWEATHER_API_KEY environment variable is required'

# Set a test API key for remaining tests
env OPENWEATHER_API_KEY=test_api_key_123

# Test basic MCP protocol initialization
exec mcp-weather-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":3}
EOF

# Verify server information in initialization response
stdout '"name":"mcp-weather-server"'
stdout '"version":"0.1.0"'

# Verify capabilities are reported
stdout '"tools"'

# Verify weather tools are listed correctly
stdout '"name":"get_current_weather"'
stdout '"name":"get_weather_forecast"'
stdout '"description":"Get current weather information for a location"'
stdout '"description":"Get 5-day weather forecast for a location"'

# Verify ping response
stdout '"result":"pong"'

# Verify tool schemas include required parameters
stdout '"location"'
stdout '"units"'
stdout '"required"'
